description: Comparison infographic in portrait format on a dark black asphalt or concrete textured background. A faint ghosted image of an open metal handcuff sits in the lower portion. The headline at top in white sans-serif reads 'Number of law enforcement officers needed to arrest a criminal alien:'. Below are two side-by-side columns introduced by colored banner labels: a red banner reading 'UNCOOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS' on the left and a blue banner reading 'COOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS' on the right, separated by a thin vertical divider. Under the red side is a large white numeral '8 to 10' with text: 'ICE needs more officers to help protect the public from unpredictable behavior by an at-large criminal attempting to evade justice.' Under the blue side is a large white numeral '2' with text: 'ICE can arrest a criminal alien with as few as two officers in a safe, controlled location, like a jail or court lockup area.' The bottom-left bears the DHS/ICE seal and 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement', and the bottom-right the URL 'ICE.gov/detainers'.
notable_text: Number of law enforcement officers needed to arrest a criminal alien: UNCOOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS 8 to 10 / COOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS 2; ICE.gov/detainers
